reasoning: Pixel art apple sprite examined at 4x and 8x zoom. Overall structure is clean with a dark 1px outline and limited palette (dark outline, orange-red body, white highlight, green leaf). Three concerns flagged: (1) Gradient shading — the body transitions from dark orange at bottom to lighter orange-salmon toward center without strict hard-tone jumps; more intermediate color steps than a hand-crafted 2-3-tone approach would use, suggesting AI interpolation. (2) Color bleed — the white highlight cluster bleeds into the orange body with 2-3 stray off-palette pixels at the boundary rather than a clean hard edge. (3) Asymmetry — right silhouette of the apple body bulges slightly more than the left when compared pixel-column by pixel-column, consistent with AI shape generation rather than deliberate pixel pushing. No sub-pixel AA detected (pixels are full-grid), no repeating pattern artifacts, no melting or semantic failure (reads clearly as an apple). Outline thickness is consistent at 1px throughout. Score 7 reflects a usable but not pristine pixel art asset.
